description: Perform a geometric quality control analysis on the Muncie HEC-RAS model. Create a Python script at ~/Documents/hec_ras_results/geometry_analysis.py that uses h5py to parse the HEC-RAS HDF5 geometry data (e.g., ~/Documents/hec_ras_projects/Muncie/Muncie.p04.tmp.hdf). The script must extract River Stations, Channel Reach Lengths, and Minimum Channel Elevations (Invert) to calculate the slope between consecutive cross-sections using the formula: Slope = (Invert_upstream - Invert_downstream) / Reach_Length. Export a CSV report to ~/Documents/hec_ras_results/reach_stats.csv with the columns: 'Upstream_RS', 'Downstream_RS', 'Reach_Length_ft', 'Upstream_Invert_ft', 'Downstream_Invert_ft', and 'Slope_ft_ft'. Ensure the HDF5 geometry data is generated via the preprocessor or simulation if it is not already available.
